findings.guard_evidence: app/app.py:248-253 — /search gates on Authorization + verify_jwt(); NOTE the JWT key is hardcoded 'secret' (app.py:26,86) so the gate is cryptographically void
findings.severity_rationale: stack/SQL-exception detail rendered to client; low rests on the info-exposure class ceiling, not the (void) auth barrier; medium arguable (leaked SQL error aids the co-located SQLi, de-facto unauthenticated)
findings.notes: co-located 2nd-order SSTI: str(e) %-formatted into render_template_string (app.py:280-281), same pattern as [3]

findings.guard_evidence: app/app.py:134 — @app.route('/register/user') has no auth decorator/check (public registration)
findings.severity_rationale: MD5 used for password hashing (fast, brute-forceable); unauthenticated; weakens every stored password
findings.notes: hashlib.md5(password) app.py:141. HEDGE: Py3 hashlib.md5(str) raises TypeError and User(username,hash_pass) uses a positional ctor db.Model doesn't define (app.py:33-36) -> route may 500 before storing; labelled real by crypto-choice intent

findings.guard_evidence: app/app.py:248-253 — /search gates on verify_jwt(); BUT the JWT key is hardcoded 'secret' (app.py:26,86) -> token forgeable in ~3 lines of PyJWT -> gate present but cryptographically void
findings.severity_rationale: user search_term %-formatted into SQL then db.engine.execute (app.py:261,265); forgeable JWT => de-facto PRE-AUTH SQLi => rubric 'pre-auth SQLi = critical' (specific beats 'past weak auth -> high'); no separate finding captures the secret, so severity is the only place it enters
findings.notes: str_query app.py:261, execute app.py:265; CodeQL src app.py:1 is imprecise (true source request.json app.py:259)
findings.true_source: app/app.py:259 content['search'] (CodeQL src app.py:1 ImportMember is imprecise)

findings.guard_evidence: app/app.py:101 — @app.errorhandler(404), reached on ANY 404 with no auth
findings.severity_rationale: request.url %-formatted into a Jinja template then render_template_string (app.py:112-114) -> SSTI/RCE; unauthenticated: GET /x?y={{7*7}} -> 404 -> RCE via the query string (path-normalization-proof)
findings.notes: render params dir/help/locals are RCE gadgets

findings.guard_evidence: app/app.py:170 — /login is the public auth endpoint
findings.severity_rationale: FP: not exploitable as reflected XSS
findings.notes: FP basis (corrected): (1) resp.mimetype='application/json' app.py:189 -> browser won't parse as HTML; (2) /login is POST-only app.py:170 -> not navigable; (3) username reaches no text/html sink anywhere. NOTE json.dumps does NOT HTML-escape (only ",\,ctrl) -> it is NOT the neutralizer

findings.guard_evidence: tests/e2e_zap.py — test harness script, not a web route (n/a)
findings.severity_rationale: FP: verify=False affects only HTTPS cert validation, but every call targets http://localhost:5050 via an http:// ZAP proxy (e2e_zap.py:9-13) -> no TLS handshake -> the flag is inert (zero security effect); test-harness/non-production is secondary
